qText: Read the following statement carefully and draw a conclusion. Statement: Most farmers in the Haryana region depend on rainwater for irrigation rather than wells and tube wells. Conclusion: I. Adequate irrigation facilities are not available in the region. II. Crop production may be affected if rainfall decreases.

Instructions: Identify the assumptions necessary to make the statement valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The discovery of new planets in habitable zones around distant stars has increased curiosity about the possibility of life beyond Earth, but great distances and limited technology make direct exploration difficult. Assumptions: I. Planets found in habitable zones may be potentially harbouring life. II. Current technology is not sufficient to directly reach such distant planets. III. If signs of life are found from afar, the need for direct exploration may be reduced.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit

qText: Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: "For the development of smart cities, it is important that equal emphasis be placed on citizen engagement and community empowerment, alongside technological innovations." Assumptions: I. Technological innovations alone are not sufficient for a successful smart city. II. Citizen engagement creates unnecessary complexity for smart cities. III. Community empowerment contributes to the long-term success of smart cities.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit
Only I and III are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: No student of Class IX failed the science exam this year. Conclusions: I. The science exam was very easy. II. All students of Class IX passed science.

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The rapid development of artificial intelligence in healthcare promises to improve diagnosis accuracy, but concerns about data privacy still hinder its full adoption. Assumptions: I. Artificial intelligence has the potential to enhance diagnostic accuracy in healthcare. II. Data privacy issues are a significant challenge in implementing AI in healthcare. III. There are no concerns related to the use of patient data in healthcare AI.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit
All I, II, and III are implicit

qText: Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement : The government has launched a nationwide campaign to promote electric vehicles in order to reduce air pollution. Assumptions : I. Air pollution is a serious problem in the country. II. Promoting electric vehicles can help reduce air pollution. III. People are willing to switch from fuel-based vehicles to electric vehicles.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only I and III are implicit
All I, II, and III are implicit

qText: Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The rapid advancement of automation and artificial intelligence could lead to significant job displacement, but it also presents an opportunity to create new jobs in emerging industries. Assumptions: I. Automation and AI could cause job losses in some sectors. II. New industries will emerge as a result of automation and AI. III. The displacement caused by automation is temporary and will be reversed by new industries.
optText: Only II and III are implicit
Only I and II are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and choose the correct Arguments. Statement: Should the minimum voting age be reduced from 18 years to 16 years? Arguments: I. Yes, today’s youth have greater political awareness. II. No, it is difficult to take mature decisions at the age of 16.
optText: Both I and II are strong
Neither I nor II is strong

qText: Instruction: Identify the assumptions necessary to make the statement valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The government has announced that the use of renewable energy in the country will be doubled in the next five years. Assumptions: I. The government has the necessary resources to increase the use of renewable energy. II. The country needs to reduce its dependence on conventional energy sources. III. The use of renewable energy will reduce environmental pollution.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit

qText: Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The introduction of artificial intelligence in education can enhance learning experiences by providing personalized tutoring, but it also raises concerns about data privacy and the replacement of human teachers. Assumptions: I. Artificial intelligence can adapt to individual learning needs. II. There are potential risks related to the misuse of student data. III. Artificial intelligence will completely eliminate the need for human teachers.
optText: Only I and II are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The city recorded the lowest air pollution levels this winter compared to the last ten years. Conclusions: I. Air quality in the city has improved over the years. II. Pollution levels in the past ten winters were higher than this year.

Statement: Despite growing interest among buyers, solar-powered home batteries remain considerably more expensive than conventional batteries, and government incentives are necessary to encourage their use. Assumptions: I. The high price is a major obstacle to widespread adoption of solar-powered batteries. II. Government incentives can make solar-powered batteries more affordable for consumers. III. Using solar-powered batteries will substantially reduce electricity costs and environmental impact.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit

qText: Statement: The city public library is open from 10 AM to 6 PM. Conclusions: I. Visitors can borrow books after 6 PM. II. The library is not accessible before 10 AM.

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement : The use of electric vehicles (EVs) in cities can significantly reduce air pollution and dependence on fossil fuels. Assumptions: I. Electric vehicles produce less pollution compared to petrol or diesel vehicles. II. A major part of urban air pollution is caused by conventional fuel-based vehicles. III. The production of electricity for EVs does not involve any pollution.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : The city recorded its highest rainfall in the last 30 years.. Conclusions : I. The city’s drainage system might have failed. II. Such heavy rainfall happens regularly.

Instruction : Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement : To effectively mitigate climate change, a global carbon tax must be implemented immediately across all industrial sectors. Assumptions : I. Current voluntary carbon reduction efforts by individual nations are insufficient to achieve significant mitigation. II. Placing a financial cost on carbon emissions will alter the economic behavior of industrial sectors. III. The technology for completely carbon-neutral energy production is already universally affordable and deployed.
optText: Only I and II are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The government has made it mandatory for all car manufacturers to install airbags in new vehicles. Conclusions: I. All new cars will have airbags. II. Airbags were not being installed in new cars earlier.

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The growing use of artificial intelligence in healthcare has the potential to greatly improve diagnostics and patient care, but it also brings up questions about privacy and data security. Assumptions: I. AI in healthcare can potentially improve diagnostics and patient care. II. Privacy and data security concerns naturally arise with advanced AI applications. III. The benefits of AI in healthcare outweigh the privacy and security concerns.
optText: Only I and II are implicit

All I, II and III are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : The bank has launched a fingerprint-based authentication system. Conclusions: I. The bank aims to enhance security. II. Customers are required to carry special ID cards.

Identify the relation between Statement I and Statement II. Statement I. The production of Kharif crops in the region decreased significantly this year. Statement II. The region received far below-average rainfall during the monsoon season.
optText: I is the cause and II is the effect.
II is the cause and I is the effect.

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The government has increased the excise duty on petrol. Conclusions: I. Now, common citizens will have to pay a higher price for petrol. II. The government's treasury is completely empty.

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: High-speed internet and digital collaboration tools have made 'remote work' a viable option for many companies, but ensuring data security and preventing cyber threats remains a major challenge. This means that for its widespread safe adoption, companies will have to invest heavily in robust cybersecurity infrastructure and employee training. Assumptions: I. The remote work environment is inherently more vulnerable to cyber threats compared to a traditional office setup. II. Employees are always less productive when working from home. III. Strong cybersecurity measures are an essential component for implementing remote work securely and on a large scale.
optText: Only I and III are implicit
Only II and III are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: After more dustbins were installed in the city's public parks, incidents of littering have significantly decreased. Conclusions: I. Providing convenience to people can bring about a positive change in their behavior. II. Citizens have now voluntarily become more aware of cleanliness.

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The successful landing of spacecraft on Mars marks a major achievement in space exploration and opens the door for future manned missions to the planet. Assumptions: I. Landing on Mars is a significant step in advancing space research. II. Future missions to Mars are being planned. III. Manned missions to Mars will face no challenges.

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The number of students opting for science stream has decreased this year. Conclusions: I. Students find science subjects difficult. II. Students may be more interested in commerce and arts.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Problem : The issue of heavy traffic jams in the main parts of the city during the morning and evening peak hours. Course of Action: Action I. The government should improve the frequency and quality of public transport (like buses and metro). Action II. The traffic police should ban the entry of private vehicles in the main areas during peak hours.

Instruction: Identify the assumptions that must hold for the statement to be valid, then choose the correct option. Statement: The Right to Information (RTI) Act is a landmark law that gives Indian citizens the right to obtain information from public authorities, thereby promoting transparency and accountability in the functioning of the government. However, this Act also includes some exemption provisions, which restrict access to certain information related to national security, strategic interests, and personal privacy. Assumptions: I. A primary objective of RTI is to bring transparency in government functioning. II. Due to the exemption provisions, the RTI Act is completely ineffective in empowering citizens. III. In a democratic governance, it is necessary to strike a balance between citizens' access to information and the nation's security interests.
optText: Only I and II are implicit
Only I and III are implicit
All I, II, and III are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The central bank has increased the interest rates on loans. Conclusions : I. The interest rates on home loans and car loans will increase. II. People will stop saving completely.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : The use of online food delivery apps has increased significantly in recent years. Conclusions : I. People are valuing convenience and time-saving more. II. People no longer like to dine out at restaurants.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All drivers must wear seat belts while driving. Conclusions : I. Drivers who do not wear seat belts are violating the law. II. Wearing a seat belt ensures safety while driving.

Public transportation can effectively reduce traffic congestion. II. Setting up advanced public transportation requires high initial costs. III. optText: Only I and II are implicit

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Millions of candidates registered for the national scholarship test this year. I. The test is widely taken and competitive. II. All candidates who registered will receive the scholarship.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: The Municipal Corporation of the city has decided to replace all old street lights installed on the roads with LED lights. Assumptions: I. LED lights will save more energy compared to old bulbs. II. The Municipal Corporation wants to enhance the city's beauty.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statements: I. The city administration has installed new sensors to monitor air quality in the city. II. The pollution level has increased in the last few months.
optText: I is the cause and II is the effect.
II is the cause and I is the effect.

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The library will remain closed on Sunday due to renovation. Conclusions: I. The renovation is planned for Sunday. II. People cannot use the library this Sunday.

Read the following statement carefully and draw conclusions. Statement: Due to the rise in gold prices in the international market, jewellery prices in the country have increased. Conclusions: I. Gold prices are influenced by the international market. II. Jewellery traders in the country are increasing prices arbitrarily.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : Increased air pollution in cities is linked to higher rates of respiratory diseases. Conclusion I: Urban residents are more prone to respiratory diseases. Conclusion II: Reducing air pollution can help decrease respiratory diseases.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All public libraries have internet access for their patrons. Conclusions : I. Patrons can use the internet for research at these libraries. II. All patrons are experts in using the internet.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: In recent years, the number of cyclists in cities has increased. Conclusion I : People are becoming environmentally conscious. Conclusion II: Car sales have stopped in cities.
Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statements : I. The government launched a road safety campaign. II. The number of accidents on the roads has increased.
optText: I is the cause and II is the effect.
II is the cause and I is the effect.
Both are effects of the same cause.

qText: Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The sale of electric vehicles is rapidly increasing in the city. Conclusions: I. People want to reduce pollution. II. Petrol vehicles have now been completely stopped.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : The use of plastic bags has been banned in many cities. Conclusions : I. Plastic harms the environment. II. People will not use any bags now.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: The government launched a new scheme to promote solar rooftop installations in urban areas. Conclusions: I. The government aims to increase the use of renewable energy sources. II. The scheme will completely replace traditional energy systems.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the action that follows. Statement: Many students find it difficult to manage time during examinations. Course of Action: 1. Conduct workshops on time management skills. 2. Cancel all examinations to reduce stress.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: Many accidents occur due to careless driving at night. Conclusions: I. Night driving should be banned. II. Drivers should be educated about safe driving.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Problem: City roads are full of potholes, leading to frequent traffic jams. Course of Action: I. The municipal corporation should repair the roads immediately. II. People should avoid using vehicles.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The bank introduced online appointment booking for branch visits. Conclusions: I. The bank wants to reduce crowding in branches. II. Customers will stop visiting branches.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: The railway department has increased the number of ticket counters at major stations. Conclusions: I. The department wants to reduce passenger queues. II. The number of passengers has increased.

Statement: To boost educational outcomes, the government has launched digital learning platforms in schools with poor performance records. Judgements: 1. Traditional teaching methods have completely failed. 2. Use of technology may help enhance students’ learning levels.

Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statements: I. Many students are enrolling in online courses. II. Online learning platforms offer flexible schedules and affordable options.

qText: Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: The prices of petrol and diesel have increased sharply in the past few weeks. Inferences: I. People may start using public transport more frequently. II. The government has decided to ban private vehicles.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the action that follows. An employee is repeatedly failing to meet project deadlines. Course of Action: 1. Give extra time and counseling. 2. Suspend the employee.

The library aims to expand its reach and attract more readers. II. The maintenance of physical books has become very expensive.
